allowed_actions.when: An existing issue owns real scope but needs a stricter boundary instead of replacement.
A later corrective milestone sharpens existing runtime or storage work without duplicating ownership.
A human-facing milestone must keep its surface while structural cleanup is handled elsewhere.
Downstream roadmap work exists but must stay blocked until the cleanup-first sequence is complete.
A future concept issue should remain non-executable and informative only.
Future milestone descriptions and issue bodies must consume the cleanup program outputs instead of perpetuating noisy patterns.

compatibility_rules: Amend or narrow the existing issue before adding a corrective milestone that consumes its scope.
Use marker-based execution-order and blocker sections so backlog edits remain machine-auditable.
Keep the local tracked source-of-truth files aligned with the live GitHub issue bodies and milestone descriptions.
prohibited_patterns: Duplicate ownership where a corrective milestone restates runtime or cleanup work without narrowing the existing issue first.
New roadmap issues without labels and execution-order status at publish time.
Using M277-B002 as a dumping ground for checker collapse, command-surface reduction, proof hygiene, or governance work.
Using M288 as a vague catch-all for structural cleanup that belongs to M313-M318.
Starting downstream runtime or future-milestone work before the cleanup-first sequence is complete.
